author: ThreeM
description: Generates and replaces images in your notes based on a description heading, using AI. - This plugin has not been manually reviewed by Obsidian staff.

manifest.author: ThreeM
manifest.description: Generates and replaces images in your notes based on a description heading, using AI.
manifest.name: Note Illustrator

name: Note Illustrator

releases.author: ThreeM-MMM
releases.description:
feat: Add optional frontmatter update (TTRPG Vault compatibility)

- Adds a new toggle setting: "Obsidian TTRPG Community Vault compatibility".
- If enabled, the plugin will now call `processFrontMatter` after image generation.
- This update targets the `Image:` (or `image:`) key in the note's frontmatter and replaces its value with the relative path to the newly generated image.
- This ensures compatibility with themes or plugins that use this key for banners or image displays.

releases.author: ThreeM-MMM
releases.description:
feat: Add customizable API endpoint and model name

Makes the plugin compatible with any OpenAI-like API.

- Adds 'API Endpoint URL' to settings (default: OpenAI).
- Adds 'Model Name' to settings (default: dall-e-3).
- Core logic now uses these settings instead of hard-coded values.
- Renamed 'Image Size' to 'Image Size (DALL-E 3)' for clarity.

releases.author: ThreeM-MMM
releases.description:
**First Release**

This is a plugin for Obsidian.md designed to help you automatically generate art for your notes. It was originally built for TTRPG Game Masters (GMs) to create images for characters and locations, but it is universally applicable for any note.

The plugin reads the text under a **user-defined heading** (e.g., `# Description`) in your active note, sends it to **OpenAI (DALL-E 3)**, and then automatically replaces a specified placeholder image in that note with the newly generated art.

**Full Changelog**: https://github.com/ThreeM-MMM/note-illustrator/commits/1.0.0
